summary: Variant normalization absent before annotation — duplicate protein entries guaranteed downstream. No hard filters applied to raw callset. Combined with proteogenomics deduplication failure, this represents a cascade failure through the entire pipeline.

findings.title: Variant normalization absent before VEP annotation
findings.message: No bcftools norm or vt normalize step between HaplotypeCaller output and VEP annotation. Without left-alignment and parsimony normalisation, the same indel can be represented in multiple non-canonical forms across samples. VEP annotation lookup may fail for non-canonical representations, and identical variants from different samples will not be recognised as equivalent in the proteogenomics database, generating duplicate protein entries for what is biologically the same substitution.
findings.impact: Duplicate protein entries inflate the search database. Multiple representations of the same indel will each generate a distinct protein sequence, multiplying the search space proportionally to the degree of non-normalisation. Downstream FDR is degraded for every duplicated variant protein. Functional annotation may be incorrect for non-left-aligned indels.
findings.recommendation: Insert 'bcftools norm -f {params.ref} -m -both' immediately after genotyping and before VEP annotation. Verify with: bcftools stats before_norm.vcf.gz vs after_norm.vcf.gz — normalisation should reduce total variant count by 5-15% for typical WGS cohorts.

findings.title: No hard filters or VQSR applied to raw variant callset
findings.message: HaplotypeCaller output is passed directly to downstream annotation without any VariantFiltration or VQSR step. Cohort size (n=12) is below the recommended minimum for VQSR (n=30 for WGS). Hard filters per GATK best practices should be applied instead.
findings.impact: Raw HaplotypeCaller callsets carry 10-30% false positive rate. Unfiltered false positive variants propagate to VEP annotation and the proteogenomics custom database, generating peptide candidates that will never be observed by mass spectrometry.
findings.recommendation: Apply GATK hard filters: QD < 2.0, FS > 60.0, MQ < 40.0 for SNPs; QD < 2.0, FS > 200.0, ReadPosRankSum < -20.0 for indels. Apply GENOTYPE_FILTER for GQ < 20.
findings.code_snippet: // GenotypeGVCFs -> VEP with no filtering step

findings.title: Duplicate marking absent from alignment pipeline
findings.message: No Picard MarkDuplicates or samtools markdup between BWA-MEM2 alignment and BQSR. PCR duplicates inflate allele depths, distorting the heterozygous/homozygous ratio in the variant callset.
findings.impact: Allele depth inflated for amplified fragments. In combination with missing hard filters (GEN-2), false confident variant calls from duplicate-inflated allele depths will pass to downstream DB construction.
findings.recommendation: Add Picard MarkDuplicates after BWA-MEM2 sort. For patterned flow cells (NovaSeq), set OPTICAL_DUPLICATE_PIXEL_DISTANCE=2500.
findings.code_snippet:
// bwa-mem2 | samtools sort -> directly to ApplyBQSR
// MISSING: MarkDuplicates
